Welcome back to Straight Line Marketing, time to dive into Module 3. Now, in this module, we're going to cover an area that is crucial to success as a marketer, namely, copywriting. Now, you probably know my story. You know that I built one of the most successful and effective sales forces in Wall Street history. In fact, to this very day, people still ask me how I managed to get all my salespeople to perform so incredibly well. I mean, these were kids with no sales experience and most of them were working odd jobs before they met me. And yet they learned the secret of becoming ridiculously persuasive. And one of the primary ways I was able to do this was through the use of scripts. And not just any script, expertly drafted, precisely engineered scripts that are so on the money that they're virtually foolproof. So in this module, you can learn how to use straight line script writing techniques when drafting your sales letters. Once you get this part right, you're going to be absolutely shocked at its effectiveness. So take out your workbook, take lots of notes here, and I'll see you on the other side. Welcome to Copywriting, and I love this module. You see, what I discovered early on here was that online copywriting was a little bit more than just script writing for the internet. I knew I was one of the best sales script writers in the world. I mean, I've been turning companies around by literally writing kick-ass sales scripts for them for the last 20 years. I knew if I could just make some minor tweaks to my highest performing sales scripts, I could then use them as scripts in written form to sell just about anything online. I mean, think about it. What is a script after all? A script is really the well thought out essence of the perfect sale. It's as simple as that. Let me repeat. A script is the well thought out essence of the perfect sales. Simple as that. So I knew if I could find a way to apply my proprietary script building technology to my online marketing deficit, I'd literally have a runaway hit. And guess what? I was right. We applied my script writing methodology to our SL fundamentals, to all the copy we put online. Our results were literally amazing. In fact, I remember sitting with Dave and going through some of the results his team had achieved. Some of their marketing clients had to switch in their copywriting efforts from the traditional methods that you see to straight line methodology. We were both really our jaws to drop. We were blown away. In this module, Dave's going to provide you guys with the method we use to integrate my proprietary script building technology with our online copywriting efforts. I'll also give you guys a few of our most effective sales scripts. These are scripts I've drafted myself. I've converted them into a template for you so it's easiest filling in the blanks and deploying. So here's Dave. Hey guys, welcome back to module three of the straight line marketing system copywriting. Now this is one of my favorite modules and in that is one of the greatest shifts or the most significant shifts that I had to actually go through one of the kind of mental shifts that I had to experience when kind of redoing or doing over my marketing efforts and approaching it in a completely different and unique way. Now this system is all based around as you know the straight line persuasion system. It's that really forms the crux of the backbone of our copywriting. And here's why you see I struggled with copywriting. I wanted to know I really wanted to be able to draft key cars high converting sales letters, but it became a challenge. I mean it was really challenging and almost overwhelming for me to be able to put together a sales letter that I was convinced and confident that would do really well. And I knew all I had to do was send some traffic to this particular sales letter and the sales will start rolling in. I mean I heard the dream. I believed in it. I just couldn't draft that kind of copy. I really couldn't put together a piece of sales copy that really produced key cars results. Now let me just rewind a bit and actually explain what copywriting is. I mean a lot of you guys are probably thinking copywriting is some kind of a highfalutin legal term and it's all about intellectual property. I know when I first got into copywriting probably about 10 years ago I've been an average student for that amount of time. That was what I first thought copywriting was. I thought it was all about intellectual property. I mean it probably has something to do with my background as a lawyer or I'm an attorney or I like to say I'm a reformer's attorney. But copywriting for me was just that. It was just like an intellectual property term. I soon discovered that copywriting was in fact an actual sales methodology. It was a system, an organized scientific system for persuading prospects to actually take a particular kind of action. Ultimately or namely we want them to become customers. So we need to actually convince someone who doesn't even know us in many cases, doesn't know anything about us or anything about our business. We need to convince them that they should purchase from us. You know that requires a significant amount of trust. They need to trust us and trust our company and love our product. But that became a real challenge for me. I couldn't do it, at least not effectively. And I studied like probably no would be copywriter or excuse me, copy cub has ever studied. I mean you name it, I've gone through it. Every kind of seminar, every piece of content ever written on the subject. I studied it because I was convinced that if I could become a master salesman in print then the world is my oyster. I could almost print money on demand but that never worked for me. And I struggled with that copy and I struggled for many years. And I spoke to Jordan about this and we had this conversation where I told Jordan look in terms of the actual technical marketing knowledge, I'm a rockstar. There's nothing about the internet I'm not completely familiar with. I understand all the little nuances. I understand how to drive traffic so effectively build sales, finals. I mean email marketing says I mean you name it. Anything that has to do with the internet, I'm on top of it. I've got it down. I'm good. But my problem is after I do everything right and I've created a really great sales funnel with so much strategy and thought behind the actual construction of the funnel and all the little nuances and you know the exit pops and I mean you name it, A, B, split test the elements and I did everything you could possibly imagine. It's just not converting. I will tell Jordan people will end up on my sales later and no matter what I did I couldn't convert them. And then Jordan said something to me and I know he's just he's already said it so it won't come as a surprise or shock to you guys but it was a powerful moment for me. He told me quite simply he said hey what's copywriting? What is it? And I told him the standard definition of copywriting is of course salesmanship in print. And he said well if it's salesmanship in print then why aren't you just taking a look at some of the highest converting sales scripts, the ones that work in the real world with real people and converting them into a sales letter. I mean we know they work. We know that they're able to convert a prospect into a customer. We know they're able to take a customer or a prospect down the straight line from open to close. I mean that's established. It's a fact. It can be evidenced empirically. I mean that's the reality. It's why don't you just take one of my sales letters, one of my sales scripts, one of my kicker scripts, the ones that have been proven timing and time out to do really well and convert it, tweak it, modify it, convert it into an online sales letter and tell me how it does. You know before even testing it the wheels in my head started turning. I started getting really excited and the reason is this. I've got so much confidence in Jordan. I mean he's really has changed my business and changed the way we do things at our agency and I've accomplished so much massive amounts of success as a product of following his instruction. But that wasn't it. I've been a student of copywriting for the longest time and I can remember at one seminar conducted by perhaps the greatest copywriter of all time, a gentleman named Gary Halberth, the late Gary Halberth he left us a few years ago I can imagine. Gary said something really interesting. Now before I actually tell you what he said let me tell you exactly who this guy is. Now this is a man that was the copywriter that ended all copywriters. He was machine when others were men. He was unbelievable. See he would come into any marketplace, take the control and just bury it. And by that what I mean copywriting speak. So the control is a package typically in those days because this was so long ago the package was actually a direct mail package so you'd have a long form sales letter along with a postcard and little bits and pieces, all physical mail and it would ultimately try to encourage a prospect to take some kind of particular action. Typically it would be filling in a prepaid envelope with your credit card details to purchase whatever weight loss supplements, whatever it is, whatever it is the copywriter is trying to sell. So what would happen is after a package has gone out and it consistently has the highest conversion levels so it consistently had more people inquire, more people take action, more people purchase the particular product that is attempting to sell it would be called the control. And other copywriters would come in and try to beat the control so they would beat it by for example changing the headline or changing some of the persuasive elements or changing the story in the copy or changing the guarantee. They would try everything they could to beat the control that is they would try everything they could to achieve a higher conversion rate have more people inquire or take the particular action that they're attempting to accomplish in that particular sales letter. Now Gary Halbert was unique in that he could move into any space, any kind of industry even though there's that completely foreign to him and those that he had little knowledge about he would just study the customer a little bit he would study the avatar and find out what makes them tick and he would apply his copywriting methodology his system and he would consistently, constantly and predictably beat the control every single time. He was in a part man part machine the guy just couldn't be beat and he was knocking out all the other copywriter's teeth and nobody could stand a chance. So I remember once there was a seminar with this guy so now you guys are familiar with copywriting I'm sure the name Gary Halbert is very familiar to you guys but those of you who may not be maybe that was your introduction to Gary and now that you know a little bit about him and just know his pedigree it will surprise you that at one particular seminar he was asked by a budding copy cub you and over again and Gary paused for a moment he looked at the student and he said you want to know do you really want to know the secret you want to know how it's done you want to know the fastest most particular business and they would act as a free agent or they would actually write the copy then take a percentage of the sales and so forth rather than actually having any stake in the business so client go to the business go straight to the sales department and have a listen to the sales team find the greatest sales person speak to the manager and tell me in every businesses like that you know you're going to have one or two sales people that are carrying the entire business that are really kicking goals and everybody else is you know barely pulling their weight frankly that's the way all businesses run so go to those kick ass sales person find the guy the guy who's really killing it does so it day in day out and record it in these days you would actually have to physically record them now the basis of the straight line persuasion system is something that we're going to need to cover right I know you've got you guys a lot of your straight line persuasion experts and those of you who aren't experts would have already gone through module one where Jordan has outlined the fundamentals of the straight line system it is imperative though that we go through it again very quickly as it pertains to copywriting so let's take a look at the straight line persuasion system and see how we're going to apply it how we're going to twist it how we're going to modify how we're going to tweak it so that it can apply perfectly and seamlessly into our copywriting efforts and we can put this stuff online and we can just we can be confident of its ability to convert prospects into customers and that's why we're all here all right so the straight line persuasion system this is of course distinct from the straight line marketing system here we're going back to fundamentals SLP not SLM now the straight line persuasion system it's a proven process for influencing controlling negotiating a sale as well as making yourself known in such a congruent way that uh in such a congruent way that others are you're compelled to take a particular action for the greater good it's the most logical elegant way to influence somebody because it cuts to the core of what really matters that's the textbook definition of the straight line persuasion system but let's take a look at it a little bit more practically now to understand the online applications of SLP we need to of course understand and appreciate the offline method a sale it has a beginning and it has an end it has an open and it has a close between the open and the close we draw one straight line right we're all familiar with that now a single straight line that indicates a sale's been open and effectively ran all the way to close now in almost every circumstance or every condition a sale's not going to go exactly how we'd like it to very rarely are we going to have a sale started open and one to close and that we didn't have to deal with any objections we didn't have to manage manage the sales process or control the sale in any way because it controls itself that's very unlikely right what we're going to have to manage every single time in the offline world and we also have to be mindful that this exact same thing occurs online is that there are going to be boundaries there's going to be boundaries on top of the straight line as well as boundaries below and the boundaries on top of the straight line and i'll show you in the next display what it is basically is when a prospect goes above the straight line we need to be able to engage them and and create enough rapport with that particular prospect we're talking about offline sales here so doing the telephone sales in particular you need to create enough rapport with that product so that they know you they like you they trust you they feel as though you're interested in them and at the same time below that straight line there's another boundary where we're supposed to be gathering massive intelligence we're supposed to be asking a lot of qualifying questions so we can learn as much about that prospect as possible right we got your poor at the top we've got your gathering intelligence down the bottom and we've got a straight line that leads from open to closed now let's take a look at that in a display right so here we are that's huge in the yellow circle right there and then we've got a straight line to the promised land which is the clothes the sales close leads massive warmth and success that's the close of our sale then we've got a boundary up the top and a boundary down below boundary down below is all that gathering intelligence asking qualifying questions and the boundary up the top it's all about developing rapport so we understand the basic sales methodology now okay we understand that we need to open a sale we understand the objective is to close them we understand that if the prospect doesn't travel perfectly down the straight line that is doesn't go from open to close seamlessly and effortlessly then we need to manage them manage or control the sale through developing rapport and gathering intelligence that's the way a sales works okay no matter what you saw we want to go through this system either consciously or unconsciously you're going to go through a similar system where you're attempting to control the sale and the prospect attempts to go out and pull the helm and speak about something that's entirely irrelevant to the sale and what do you do you have to drive them back into the straight line typically by asking the questions relating to the sale in that case you're gathering intelligence you're driving them back onto the straight line and you're having them like you and trust you through body language and tonality you're developing rapport the objective is of course to keep the prospect on that straight line this is where things get powerful so I think it's fitting and I think it's appropriate for me to give you a quick caution now the straight line system is a super powerful tool you all know at the straight line persuasion system you can convince just about any body to do just about anything but this system is designed to close not only those who are closable but also those who ought to be closed people who you should be closing so I don't want you guys to be using this system you know haphazardly just writing some really persuasive sales stuff and using these secrets using some of the elements we're going to be going through throughout this module and really delivering that message to a market or to a prospect who you ought not to be delivering it to because quite frankly you're going to make the sales you know I don't know whether that's a good thing or a bad thing I guess it's good in that it really does it really provides testament to the effectiveness of the system but I'm a little uncomfortable with it because it does empower a lot of people and perhaps empower is the long choice of word but it does allow people to sell products or services, go to services to prospects who may not be the right fit who you shouldn't be selling it to and I mean the straight line marketing system isn't about that it's about creating an enormous amount of wealth it's about really kicking goals kicking ass breaking records but doing it all ethically you know and I want you guys to be mindful of that okay so there are there are unconscious buying triggers and certain patterns that you can run language patterns in the case of copywriting or tonal patterns in the case of regular sales that can make people buy things that they ought not to buy they shouldn't buy so if it's used in the wrong way it can be dangerous it's unethical something that I don't support and it's something I know Jordan doesn't support very seriously that's one of Jordan's mantras now you know it's all about doing the selling the right thing to the right person money's not not the objective the objective is to add as much value as you can to your prospects life the money will come with it I promise you so be mindful of that don't use any of what we're about to teach you for anything other than empowering the right people to purchase the right things and adding to their adding to the value excuse me to their lives as a result of having done that a little bit more caution therefore it's important that you remain ethical don't pressure a client to do something they shouldn't do ever and by client we also mean prospect of course and never pressure a client to buy something they shouldn't buy just don't do it it's not worth it it's not worth the headaches it's not worth the refund rates it's not worth not being able to look at yourself in the mirror in the morning we'll be able to sleep at night it's not worth it just because you can do it doesn't mean you should sure it doesn't mean you ought to I promise you okay there's going to be enough people who really want your product especially if you have a great kick ass product we enough people want you don't need to sell to people who shouldn't have it you just don't need to and it's going to cause you more pain than good so use your new copywriting powers ethically use them for good rather than evil all right now when we're drafting copy when we're putting together a sales letter no sales teacher it's going to explain this and this should be one of the only things you need to learn when you're drafting copy it's at least one of the key things if not the single key chief thing that ought to be taught you can't effectively close a sale without establishing the three 10s you can't do it it can't happen it if you are able to close you had a lay down you had somebody that would have closed no matter what you said or did so you shouldn't be proud of your copy you shouldn't be proud of how well you're able to convert a prospect if they were able to convert without you having successfully and in great detail and without leaving any stone unturned establishing the three 10s it's mandatory it's must okay so what are the three 10s one they must love your product this is where copywriting begins and ends okay traditional copywriting I mean a copywriting is all about selling a particular product so a champion copywriter or an ostensibly champion great copywriter will stand in front of a class and he'll teach his his copycups how to draft an awesome sales letter and it focuses exclusively on the product he or she is trying to sell talk about features you'll talk about benefits they'll have to guarantee they might add some scarcity and build a great story with an awesome headline to attract attention and so forth but it's all around the product now what have they forgotten they've forgotten two and three remember our straight line system we have to establish a 10 in in the prospects and attitude towards your product they have to love your product then we have to ensure that that prospect trusts and connects with us so your sales copy has to have some awesome copy or some awesome awesome content around you around the person who's trying to sell to them now this could be it I'm actually going to show you some examples okay as we move throughout this course so there's probably better I leave that to the examples but just be mindful of that reality you need to want to establish a 10 with respect to how the prospect thinks about you they've got to trust you they've got to connect with you your copy needs to have that and then finally they need to trust and connect with your company now depending on what you sell two and three could become interchangeable and I operated in markets where I needed two and I needed three for instance we would have a product where we're actually selling a particular information product and there was an expert and it was being sold by a company so in that case we needed to sell the actual product so the content that the expert was going to deliver the prospects we had to sell the expert he saw so then the prospect needed to trust and connect with this particular expert and then they needed to trust and connect with the company of course that was the training company that was putting on the product putting on the training so in many cases you'll have to do all three in some cases you have to do one and two in some cases you'll have to do one and three depending on your business and we'll look at a few different examples but remember three tens almost always need to be satisfied okay and if you look at great copy where great copywriters will say oh yeah this is a brilliant piece of copy you know I use this element and that element and so on and so forth you know I had an awesome headline that really attracted attention right away in the first three or four seconds which of course as you know is one of Jordan's Jordan's sales mantras you've got to catch the attention across within three or four seconds that's what a headline does but they might say something like that and then they say we had a great pre-head that had credibility to whatever was coming next and then I had a great subhead that added further credibility and a great lead that sucked in the prospects they had to keep on reading the entire sales letter and they're convinced that their reason for their success with were all these copywriting elements whereas I would look at the copy and I'd say no that copy that piece of copy was winner I mean that beat the control because you satisfy the three tens and you don't even know it so you have a lot of great copywriters real kick-ass copywriters who are doing just this and they're doing it unconsciously or subconsciously you don't even know they're doing it and if you guys are actually aware and cognizant of the reality that the reason a lot of copy does well is because they satisfy the three tens and the copy that doesn't do well you you are invariably going to look at it and say okay well there you go you haven't satisfied two and three you focused on one you focus on the product you didn't sell yourself you didn't sell the company you'll notice yourself that you're able to identify good copy from bad copy whereas the actual author of the copy or the copywriter himself or herself isn't able to tell you why the copy was successful or unsuccessful so you're already leagues ahead of trained professional copywriters here's a concept that you need to be able to apply to every piece of a copy that you write from here onwards unless the action thresholds Jordan's the only person in the world that'll teach you this it will make an enormous amount of difference if you actually visualize what the action threshold is now the traditional academic definition of the action threshold is an unconscious set of points that somebody has to be motivated to before taking action okay they need to be motivated past their action threshold before they're prepared to pull out their credit card and buy whatever it is you're selling so think of the action threshold in two different ways okay one way to think of it as it is as an invisible line and once you've crossed that line you know you're in bi-territory you can ask for the order because you've satisfied the three tens you've done so effectively and efficiently use some of the copywriting elements we're going to speak about in the rest of this module throughout the rest of this module and you know it's go time you can ask for the order you can put a buy button there because they're ready to buy you're not going to put a buy button before you pass the action threshold we're going to look at a lot of long form sales letters and you're never going to see a buy button up the top even by even by marketers who don't know these stuff instinctively and intuitively they know I can't ask a prospect to buy anything right now because I haven't sold I haven't effectively sold my product that's what an untrained internet market is going to be thinking like now you guys have some you guys are able to flesh out that what most people do intuitively and you're able to actually systemize it because you understand the concept of the action threshold and the reason why you look at a long form sales letter and you never see a buy now button or a cta or a call to action leave your email address whatever it is they're trying to accomplish in the long form sales letter right at the top is because they know intuitively instinctively subconsciously that they haven't encouraged the prospect or they haven't effectively passed the prospects action threshold the threx they're below that action threshold and until they go above it you're you haven't earned the right to ask for a sale be mindful of that when you're drafting your copy keep the action threshold in your mind ask yourself the question have I satisfied with three tens have I effectively controlled this sale have I applied the six elements that we're going to be speaking about further on in this module and I've done so effectively have I done so effectively and if you can't answer that question without resounding yes you have no right to ask the prospect to purchase you shouldn't even attempt it okay you can only hurt yourselves asking for say asking for a purchase prior to reach reaching or passing the prospects action threshold that's why if you guys can remember back in module one we spoke about vsls and those of you who remember the vsl module you'll remember I said about excuse me module two right online fundamentals was the last module and you'll notice that I spoke about the vsls and I said something about that magic buy button and that was magic button button was a timed buy button so people would watch an actual video sales letter and they weren't actually presented with the opportunity to click the buy now button or the add to cart button until a particular time it was timed right and all the controls the vsl were hidden from the prospect so he or she didn't know what was happening all they had was a presentation in front of them and they couldn't even buy if they wanted to until the buy button magically appeared it was a hidden hidden buy button and the reason we purposely timed that buy button up until the end of the vsl just before the end of the vsl is because we know that we haven't passed the bias action threshold yet and if we put that button there and we allow the prospect to click the buy button buy buy buy now button excuse me or the add to cart button and go into the going to the actual billing section or the checkout section and take a look at the price and take a look at what they're going to be getting in the course and and all the other information we know we can do nothing but hurt our chances for sale okay so we need to wait to the action threshold has been satisfied and passed before we can ask for a sale and that's why we do it for the vsls because we have the ability to configure that buy button through some through a very simple piece of code but i'm going to teach you guys how to do this don't continue yourself with that right now the but what the big picture thing is we don't allow prospects to see the button because they haven't passed the action threshold yet in a long form sales letter that is a sales letter that is in long form format so it's a long sales letter that you have to scroll down to before you can actually see the purchase now button or add to cart button we notice that the button is all the way the end of the sales letter right you never see the button up the top and i'm sure you're all nodding now and saying yep i'm familiar with those kinds of letters and i know that i've never seen a button up the top and now you guys know why whereas the person who's actually crafted that sales letter the marketers who have put together that funnel or the copywriter who's written the actual sales letter they themselves don't know why they done it they just know it works right it works because they've tested it they've tested putting that buy button everywhere everywhere you can imagine above the fold below the fold all the way at the end of the sales letter and they know through empirical testing that it works best at the end but we know we know a straight line marketers that the reason it works so well at the end of the sales letter as opposed to at the beginning or the middle is because we haven't passed the bias or prospects action threshold now i said there was one way of looking at the action threshold right in a visible line we have to clear prior to clearing it you have no right to watch the sale after clearing it go for it you've compensated three times used all the appropriate and required set copy or sales elements in your actual piece of persuasive copy and you've earned the right to ask for a sale so put your buy button in there ask them for the sale asking for their email address asking for whatever you're trying to have them accomplish as long as it's after that invisible action threshold line now the other way i want you guys to look at the action threshold and this is the way it was first visualized to me by jordan and jordan taught me this concept privately at his place when we were talking about the action threshold which i thought was such a such a powerful concept the idea of visualizing the point in time where it can actually start asking prospects for money i thought it was brilliant and he visualized it a little differently or he provided a little different display he recommended i looked at it as a scale now let's take a look at it so he's jordan's idea or representation of the action threshold here we go you've got a scale on the left hand side you've got positives on the right hand side you've got negatives once the positives can outweigh the negatives so once negatives are basically all the reasons they shouldn't buy right that's their objections their objections in themselves their objections in you their objections in the product your objections with respect to the company with with respect to their beliefs about buying and sales people and and online marketing and sales letters and you know hype and and all the rest of it they have all these negative beliefs right and they have all these my rings that are holding them back and they're the negatives right and on the left side which is the positive side we have all the reasons they should buy so here we've got um you know great things about you great things about your company your guarantee that you yourself stand by us in addition to the guarantee provided by provided for by the company we're talking about um how great the product is how how many people have had success with the product and we're talking about all the positives associated to not only your product but um associated related to business with you do doing business excuse me with you and with your company now Jordan's idea is once the positives outweigh the negatives that's it you've hit the atrium threshold you've passed that invisible line it's go time ask for the sale and man go through copy read as much awesome copy as you can now i've got three of my favorite pieces of copy that have nothing to do with the straight line marketing system and i purposely compared them against a group or a bunch of sales scripts not provide you access to both of them so if you guys go into your resource area please don't do it now do it at the end of the module but i'll tell you about it now to give you guys persuasion system okay guys so we have the straight line persuasion system going from open to close and what you're doing is you're working to cross the action threshold by knocking out negatives as we've explained and building up positives and in doing so you've changed the beliefs of the prospect not only the beliefs about you or your product or your company but perhaps even more importantly beliefs about themselves about their own ability and beliefs about sales online reading sales that are online people may think that the idea of reading along from sales letter online is hyping and spamming you need to be able to manage that objection as you're building up positives knocking out negatives passing the press prospecting through that action threshold and as a result you've earned going to break your own records you're going to smash industry records you're going to you're better equipped at drafting copy than professional copywriters and that's a complete guarantee I say that in complete sincerity and honesty you just simply can't go wrong all right guys we've reached a turning point in this particular module in that now I'm confident that everybody here has a sound and solid understanding of the straight line persuasion system as it pertains to copywriting as well as it relates to copywriting and how it influences our abilities to convert prospects when we draft or when we present the sales letters that we've draft it's time now to speak about or share with you guys some of the secrets and some of the steps we use when we're drafting or crafting our own sales letters and I can share with you now guys that our sales letters later excuse me convert like gangbusters they really do just have some fantastic conversion rates and the reason we're able to convert remember this is all of course done within the guidelines presented to us or afforded to us through the straight line persuasion system so be mindful of that that the straight line persuasion system of course if any of what we say or any of these steps or when you're crafting your sales letter you notice that one of the steps requires you to maybe go too far off the straight line and we know as straight line marketers that we need to stay on the straight line and there are boundaries above and below that line and if we go off into Pluto we've lost control of the sales so if you find yourself in a situation like that be mindful that the straight line fundamental rules will always prevail okay so if you feel as though any of the steps act to the in conflict with some of these straight line principles that you're now familiar with the straight line principles will prevail in the case of any kind of conflict be mindful of that that's a good test for you guys or a good litmus test to whether or not your strategy your copy is actually um serviceable and if it actually adheres to all these straight line principles then it's a good chant that you can put your copy out there online put it up on a webpage and it'll do just fine before you guys write a single word there's a few things I want you guys to do that's before you put pen to paper or before you start striking any keys in your keyboard there are a few things that I need you guys to do and that largely revolves around research okay we want you to know exactly who your audience is and by that what I mean is not simply somebody who for instance if you're a hairdresser somebody who wants a haircut or wants their hair styled not good enough I need you guys to all create a customer avatar and by that what I mean is just a fictional character a person that you've invented or created in your own mind that fits the bill or the mold of your perfect customer and that could be as simple or as detailed as you like as long as it has some of the key features that we require when we're drafting copies going to make it so much easier for you guys to put your sales letters together if you know who exactly you're talking to because now you guys know because you're straight line marketers you're not an average internet marketer who isn't familiar with the signs of the straight line persuasion technology you know that a sales letter or a piece of copy is a sales conversation so don't you think it'll help to have a good idea of who exactly you're having that conversation with of course it can only help so what I want you guys to do is create a perfect customer avatar and that avatar could be something like for instance you know you're a hairdresser you know your average and ideal client is say between 24 and 34 they're married or they're single they're educated or they're uneducated they're brunettes or they're blonde they have long hair short hair they like to get their hair cut every week or every month I mean just let your imagination run wild on this one try to create an avatar give it a name and really write your copy to that one person your ideal client it's going to make an enormous difference to me in the way your copy sounds in the way it reads and the impact it has on the readers or on your prospects and not only that once you actually put your avatar together it's going to have an enormous impact on your abilities of bi-traffic which is something we're going to speak about in the later module but remember traffic is all about targeting we need to target the right people the people who are likely to be able to satisfy the requirements of the 310s against rights we need to be able to target our traffic and buy our traffic and target it to somebody or to a prospect who's likely to love our product love and trust us like and trust our company and you're you're going to be able to know that information you're going to be able to target effectively and efficiently after you've created a customer avatar you know exactly who you're going to target for our purposes now though what I'd like you guys to do is create that avatar create that fictional customer and it could even be based around one of your past customers right so you could have a past customer and that could be your avatar and write all your copy to him or her now I want you to try find the three biggest pain points of that particular avatar so if you've created a customer a fictional prospect you want to be able to use your imagination use your creativity use your market research use competitive intelligence to try to determine the three biggest pain points experienced or suffered by that prospect okay so write that down you guys will notice there's a there's a company workbook or in the resource section where you can actually where you're given space to write all these answers down I want you to take advantage of that so right now you've listed who your target market is who exactly you're planning on speaking to you've listed or identified a few characteristics to be able to identify that person and you've also made clear they're three biggest pain points okay so if you're selling a weight loss pill the biggest pain point could be something like can't lose weight because they like chocolate too much they've tried every other diet it's failed they can't exercise because they have bad knees now it's just riffing gears off the top of my head but you guys get the idea we're listing the three biggest pain points and you're putting it into your workbook now here's where you're going to need to really put your thinking caps on because I want you to list your magic answer to their problems now that is one problem it's your magic bullet solution you're going to have so many features for your products no matter what it is you're selling they're going to be countless features and along with those features are going to be countless numerous benefits but right now I want you to think of that one magic bullet that one thing that's likely to solve most of all of their problems okay and write that down in your workbook okay we're going to come back and all these little pieces that you've written down all this work you've done are going to be then used to assemble your sales letter because I don't ever like to think of sales copy or as writing copy or drafting copy although I use those terms when I think about it I really just like to think of it as though I'm assembling a sales letter because I have all the little pieces I know who I'm talking to I know my cross-bets biggest problems I know there's some of their their fears and problems of terms that can be used interchangeably in this case I know that I've got an awesome solution a real magic bullet answer that solved their problem I've written that down and it's a matter of piecing all these little pieces and add these elements together and then drafting our sales letter and you'll see it all come together the next part I want you guys to do is write down the three most likely objections your prospect will raise after having been presented with the solution okay so you've given them a great solution you've told them a bit about your product what are they likely to say are they likely to say it's a little too expensive are they likely to say I've tried everything else why would your product be any different are they likely to say something like why would I trust a company like yours with my mortgage and with my free mortgaging or financing I would much rather trust a more reputable bank or company that I've heard about before I've never heard about your company before right so what are the three most likely objections you would encounter after having presented your fictional prospect in this case your avatar the character you've come up with in your own mind what are the three most likely objections that you would expect to face and those of you who actually been selling for a while will really know the objections you wouldn't need to do thinking because you would be hearing them every day but those of you who aren't actually there on the phones every day or aren't you involved in sales on a regular basis may need to use a little bit of creativity you could also interview past prospects and find out some of their key objections to people who hadn't purchased before or you can go on to forums and do your research but I want you guys to be confident and clear on the three most likely objections that a prospect will raise once presented with your offer now here's the formula so well that was before we've written a single word right so that was all pre-copy now we're going to talk about the formula now of course this is all composed or drafted or assembled in light of the straight line persuasion system right so we all know the straight and straight line fundamentals which is why we covered them in pre-decental or decent detail at the start of this module so in light of all of that this is how you put together a sales letter that converts like gangbusters step one you need to have your features right you need to write your features down again write them down in the workbook provided and then we want to write your benefits we're gonna go through each of these guys so so don't get don't get a little bit nervous but just rattling them off now flying through them then we'll go through each of them write down your benefits then put together or craft a story a story as it relates to your particular product then put together a summary then I want you to write a headline and finally a call to action so let's go through those steps really quickly and believe me after having just done your pre-copy stuff your sales letter is almost rewritten and you would have a pretty great sales letter that's going to convert higher than anything you've had online before all right features features are what's inside your product it's what it consists of so if you've if you're selling a book what are the features the features of the chapters if you're selling a car what are the features the features of the wheels the steering wheel the exhaust the color these are all features okay this is what your product consists of now this is distinct of course from benefits and the sales people among us will know this this is sales 101 there is a clear distinction between features and benefits let's just talk about benefits now and that'll even help us understand features okay now benefits are what makes the features that we've just identified in step one so exciting and useful in the prospect now a good trick or tip in determining what a benefit is you can simply ask yourself so what so list of benefits for instance the color of the car is red so what will naef think well why is the color red so great I mean how is that possibly a benefit to the prospect you could say oh because this is a limited edition race car color there are only four of them all the other color all the other cars have come off the assembly line in black this is an exclusive car color there are only a certain amount and you can add exclusivity you can add scarcity so what you've done there is you've turned a feature which is just what the product consists of into a benefit something that excites the prospect so now you're familiar with the so what this okay so you just take a feature ask yourself so what and try to convert it into a benefit another way you can convert a feature into a benefit is by simply saying to yourself after having listed a benefit or identified a benefit okay so what that means to my prospect is taking the same analogy we've got a car and it's in red and then you would say to yourself so what that means to my prospect is it means that they're part of an exclusive group or a handful of drivers who have access to this particular red car or red color car where all the other colors are blacks they have certain amount of exclusivity that all you've done is you've asked yourself what that means to my prospect is and then you've answered your own question it's as simple as that that's how you convert a feature into a benefit you either ask yourself so what and then come up with a pretty great answer or if it's easier ask yourself and what that means to my prospect is nice and simple after you've listed all your benefits we're going to move into step three which is our story step now there are a lot of benefits to a story but it also comes with a significant caution now remember the straight line the straight line persuasion fundamental rule is to keep your prospect on the straight line if you draft or assemble the sales letter with a story component which i recommend everybody does because what a story doesn't sucks the prospect in and they can't help but continue to read the rest of the sales letter because they want to find out what happens in the story right so it's a vehicle for eliciting emotion from your particular prospect and you can use that emotion to drive them down the straight line so i'm a big believer in stories but the reason i caution all of you guys and it comes with a strong warning is that the story cannot take the prospect off the straight line you can't lose control of the sale the story needs to further the sale all right so for that story to earn its place on your sales letter on your long form sales letter or on your bsl that story needs to be able to be used as a vehicle to further travel the prospect down the straight line so if you want to come up with come up with a story that that's really great and interesting and fascinating and you feel as though it will encourage the reader to continue to read the sales letter but it does nothing to further the sale it does nothing to control the sale it sends the prospect over out into Pluto then we need to drop it don't worry about the story remember the rule where the typical formula and the straight line fundamental principles conflict the straight line fundamental principles will always prevail okay so that's your that's your guideline okay that's going to help you craft draft or assemble a perfect sales letter be mindful of the importance of the straight line principle but if you do a story correctly and not only will it suck a prospect didn't get them excited interested to read the rest of your sales letter but it also has the added impact and effect of actually furthering the sale further controlling it and driving them further down the straight line here's how you put your story together now what you can guys can do you can write a story about yourself it obviously depends on your product you can write a story about a successful customer so customers have had a great experience with your product in the past and just make it very emotional and thought-provoking and really get the prospect excited that remember that your story should always further the sale in that it should either continue to sell your product and or continue to sell your company and or continue to sell you and your services so that the customer prospect trusts and likes you loves your product trusts and likes your business so that in mind here's our story formula first you want to start with the problem here list down the problem you guys have already done that in your pre in your pre copywriting stage right you found a problem say in this case a problem can be our prospect needs to lose weight let's stick with the lose weight analogy right and then part two excuse me it's only a there's only a four part the worry and I know that part three is missing right so it should be one two three and four and although we've got one two four and five so I'm sorry about that so you've got your problem in part one in this case the problem is that our prospect needs to lose weight right part two is why the problem could never be solved okay let's come up with an intractable problem with this particular prospect in this case we'll say that the prospect can't lose weight because the prospect loves chocolate okay he's absolutely addicted he's a real chocoholic can't get enough chocolate that's why he or she could never lose the weight okay now we're going to talk about your magic solution remember your magic solution that one bullet that really big thing that's going to solve the problem now that magic solution that we've come and that we've come up with in our pre-copy in this case could be something as simple as you sell a supplement a shake for instance that is chocolate flavored that tastes exactly like real chocolate like Cadbury chocolate you can't tell the difference that's the solution and it will get you full and it has no calories and no carbohydrates and it will help you lose weight right it's your magic solution now what are the results of your solution okay so the results of the solution is this particular prospect could never lose weight before because of her strong chocolate addiction or his strong chocolate addiction they finally had found your product which is this fantastic delicious chocolate tasting shake they've been consuming it two or three times a day they've been full and satisfied all day and for the first time ever they've finally lost all the weight that they wanted to lose they've hit their target goals they couldn't be happier and they're much healthier and glad they took advantage of your product right so that's a basic story we had a problem we talked about why the problem was intractable it couldn't be solved we talked about your solution or the solution that your product offers and then we talked about the results after having experienced or taken advantage of your magic solution it's as simple as that that's how we craft a great story and you'll notice of course stories can be so complicated they can be over 20 20 25 pages and in the few of the copywriting examples i'm going to share with you guys at the end of this module you're going to notice a few stories i want you guys to identify the stories yourself rather than having them identified for you and see if you can spot the formula see if you can spot a problem see if you can spot why the problem couldn't be solved see if you can can't find a magic solution that's being offered by the copywriter or all the business and see whether or not if you can't find the results of a prospect having taken advantage of that solution now we're going to talk about our summary stack up the benefit for a big bang now by that i mean it at the end of your sales letter right you're going to have a whole bunch of benefits a whole bunch of features you're going to have you know a great story you're going to have all these great elements but they were so far up in the sales letter that by the time you're actually getting close to the cta you're getting close to asking for the order a lot of it's escaped the mind of the prospect right a lot of people have been forgotten even if the prospect is really diligent you know they were sucked into the sales letter they just couldn't get enough of it they would have forgotten a lot of benefits what i'd like to do is at the end of the sales letter say don't forget you're getting this and you're getting that and you're getting that and you're going to and what that means for you yes i've just listed all the features you know you're gonna get this great car you know it's got air conditioning it's got power steering i'm listing all the features and then i'm going to back it up with benefits and what that means to you is you know we said have air conditioning what that means to you is you know i'm super fast called air enjoy those hot summer days cruising around in you in your nice cool car highest safety ratings with this power steering a smooth effortless ride and so forth right it's just beating off the top of my head but you guys get the point so you're list all the features again list all the benefits stack them on top of each other right at the end of the sales letter remind them of the stories remind them of testimonials if need be remind them of a guarantee and and really just pack make sure that final summary of your sales letter packs a powerful punch and they don't forget any of what you explain to them throughout the course of the sales letter right now the headline counter culturally i don't start with the headline i always end with the headline and the reason i end with the headline the headline is in many ways a slow process i don't like coming up with a headline right away i like actually writing my sales letter all of it finding identifying all my benefits having a look at my story taking a look at my features taking a look at my summary going through the entire sales letter everything that i've done and then i'll then i just hope that instinctively or intuitively a great headline comes to mind doesn't always happen that easy as sometimes it does and sometimes you just written your sales letter you're really happy with it and the headline just pops into your head and you just know that's helping right but it doesn't happen very often but when it does it's great but when you're drafting a sales letter excuse me when you're putting together your headline you want to remember Jordan's three-second rule remember copywriting your salesmanship multiple salesmanship in print right so what we need to do is be mindful of how we typically start a phone call if we're running a regular phone call we speak to a regular phone call or we're excuse me making a regular sales call that first three seconds that where Jordan says first first three seconds you need to be sharp as a tactic you need to be enthusiastic it's hard we need to be an expert in your field you can't lose them that first three seconds is vital that first three seconds is your headline right because that your prospect is going to take a look at that headline and you've got three seconds or less to suck them in and get them interested right you have three seconds now not always is your product an information product or a product where you really need to establish your authority now if you do need to establish your authority like you would in most sales calls it's absolutely mandatory and imperative that the prospect knows your sharpest tactic enthusiastic curl and an expert in your field but sometimes that isn't required we're going to go through examples where it is and where it isn't sometimes it makes more sense to put together a headline that actually gets the prospect excited about the sales letter as opposed to getting the prospect excited and confident about you and your product just yet okay so be mindful of that difference that that's one of these smaller nuances that separate regular traditional copywriting from regular traditional sales but if you're in the financial space or you know or insurance or anything like that you'll notice that most of the highest performing sales letters always establish that either the expert if the the copy if the copy is written by or presented as though it was written by an expert is sharp as a tactic the asti color than the expert in his or her field or that the company itself is a very intelligent well-stayed company it's very caring and enthusiastic and they're just experts at whatever it is they do the same rules apply and we're going to take a look at the examples where they do the second resource I want to give you or the second tip I want to give you when crafting your headline is don't reinvent the wheel take a look at some of the highest converting headlines ever and to do so all we have to do is go on to dig.com this diwg.com and there you're going to see a collation of some of the most viral articles right some of the most viral pieces of content and these are content that grab virtually everybody's attention and everybody who saw that saw it was interested in that's clicked it shared it on social media that just was super viral super significant everybody loved it and think about it the only reason people are going to share it and a piece of content is going to go viral is if they actually clicked on it right they can't have a look at the content without clicking on it and the only thing that they would have seen prior to clicking is the headline so take a look at those headlines and take a look at what's really trending and and have a look at and help you get some ideas on you'll go through dig.com and you'll see the famous headlines like that seven tricks to seven steps to you may look at something like that and say man I can really apply that to my sales letter so you can go on to dig.com use it for creativity inspiration try to find the great headlines the headlines that get everybody interested and excited because remember you've got three seconds to get your prospect interested in whatever it is you're writing and the only way you're going to do that is by crafting or drafting or writing headlines that have been proven to get attention over and over again one of the best ways to do that is simply to go on to dig.com I can give you a list of some of the highest converting headlines in the past but the problem with that is not and I had a templated list of some awesome headlines all the way from the from the 80s or something starting back in the 20s but the ones I really focused on were the new one from the 80s and onward till today and what I noticed was that even though they had worked in the past for some reason they stopped working people weren't interested in them anymore they weren't the kind of headlines that got people to click on an ad they weren't the kind of headlines that encourage people to read the rest of the sales letter and that's because this copyrighting isn't art not a science or it is an art but one that can be guided by a certain amount of science but isn't itself a science and by that I mean it's not timeless right the principles are but the actual techniques the kinds of headlines you use the kinds of stories you use that change with time and they always do so I can't use a headline that was really kicking us in the 90s or early 2000s and try to use that headline and apply it to my sales copy today because it just won't work which is why a resource like dig.com is really good because it shows you the content that's trending now it shows you the kinds of headlines that people are interested in and clicking on now and if you go on to dig.com and have a look around you're going to have so many ideas I mean some of them may not be very useful because they're celebrity based like Kim Kardashian's Secret or whatever but you'll notice that even from that headline you say okay well I've got a celebrity and we have their secrets so I can think of say a celebrity that provided of course it's within the advertising guidelines and it's all above board you can say you know a celebrity secret to losing weight and you may have a weight loss supplement that was used by a particular celebrity or or a an ingredient in a supplement that was used by a celebrity that you could leverage you could use this headline similar to that provided your advertising guidelines allow you to do so so just be so you let your creativity run wild go on dig.com try to find some of the best headlines ever don't worry too much about the content okay because it's written in a very different style the the articles written in article based format not in copywriting format not in sales formats don't worry too much about the content but pay a lot of attention to the headlines you'll get some great ideas and you'll get some some ideas that you can constantly refer back to and you'll notice that the actual tone of headlines change over time and and that's just testament to the reality that people's people aren't always interested in one particular one particular way of signifying or identifying something right so a headline that may have worked really well in the past there's a good chance it won't work well now and you'll notice that as you go through dig.com then keep an eye on it and you can see the trend the trending tonality in those headlines step six is the call to action now the call to action is simply asking for the order now um i know Jordan's a big believer in that he said he's preached that most sales actually fail not because there was anything wrong with the actual sale i mean they controlled the sale they kept them on the straight line they were able to ask great qualifying questions and they were able to develop massive rapport through body language and tonality and establish the three teams and everything moved along perfectly they circumnavigated around objections through looping and everything was brilliant but the sale never closed and the reason it never closed in a in a circumsensor a condition where all the other elements were satisfied is because the salesperson simply didn't ask for the order it's one of the biggest reasons sales sales fails an actual sales call will fail because the salesperson just waits for the prospect to come up with a reason why the call has to end right it didn't have the guts to ask for the order now i remember Jordan was um being interviewed by um my god it was a Larry King or or Pierce Morgan it was a major major interview and i would speak to Jordan to what did it do for business i mean that was a huge interview a huge crowd i mean Jordan looked at me like i'd kick the baby we did nothing for business because there was no cta there was no call to action i didn't ask anybody throughout the interview to do anything and he's absolutely right i mean you can drive as much traffic as you like to a sales funnel you can drive as much traffic you like to a uh to a web page or to a sales page right and you guys can have a kick our sales letter if you don't have a call to action if you don't ask the prospect to do something after having landed on that page just like Jordan didn't ask the prospect asked the viewers to do anything after being interviewed by a major television personality and having millions of people tune in to watch the interview nothing's going to come of it right no matter how much you spend on traffic no matter how many viewers you have to the television channel nothing will come of that activity if you didn't actually ask for the sale so in the case of an opt-in page what are you going to sales funnels eat that is just it changes it will change your business there is no two way about it a sales funnel has the potential of extracting the most amount of value from every customer that you come in contact with that means a customer for you will be likely worth more than that same customer would be worth to your competitors which means of course that you can spend more money than your competitors to acquire that customer and once you can do that you know that you can spend more money than your competitors to actually pick up the customer then you can grow at whatever rate you want to grow you know the sky is your limit you can grow as fast as you like you can take as much market share as your heart desires because you've developed a mathematical relationship between the acquisition of customers and the cost to your business and provided that the benefits attached to acquiring that customer outweighs the cost associated with acquiring that same customer then you're going to be in the green for every customer you acquire hopefully at point of sale if not you definitely develop the ability to at least profit through the lifetime value of the customer now that may sound a little convoluted I may be getting a little ahead of myself I'm just really excited about the module that's coming up but it's one of my favorites so gear up get ready for the sales funnel module once you actually learn how to develop a awesome kick ass sales funnel it is virtually impossible to do business the way you have been doing in the past it changes everything so I look forward
